feed.title: blank
feed.subtitle: A simple, whitespace theme for academics. Based on [*folio](https://github.com/bogoli/-folio) design.

entry.title: [arXiv] SalUn: Empowering machine unlearning via gradient-based weight saliency in both image classification and generation.

link.title: [arXiv] SalUn: Empowering machine unlearning via gradient-based weight saliency in both image classification and generation.

entry.content:
Figure 1. Schematic overview of comparing conventional unlearning methods with our proposal (SalUn) in the context of mitigating the influence of the harmful concept of ‘nudity’ in DM.

Abstract

With evolving data regulations, machine unlearning (MU) has become an important tool for fostering trust and safety in today’s AI models. However, existing MU methods focusing on data and/or weight perspectives often grapple with limitations in unlearning accuracy, stability, and cross-domain applicability. To address these challenges, we introduce the concept of ‘weight saliency’ in MU, drawing parallels with input saliency in model explanation. This innovation directs MU’s attention toward specific model weights rather than the entire model, improving effectiveness and efficiency. The resultant method that we call saliency unlearning (SalUn) narrows the performance gap with ‘exact’ unlearning (model retraining from scratch after removing the forgetting dataset). To the best of our knowledge, SalUn is the first principled MU approach adaptable enough to effectively erase the influence of forgetting data, classes, or concepts in both image classification and generation. For example, SalUn yields a stability advantage in high-variance random data forgetting, e.g., with a 0.2% gap compared to exact unlearning on the CIFAR-10 dataset. Moreover, in preventing conditional diffusion models from generating harmful images, SalUn achieves nearly 100% unlearning accuracy, outperforming current state-of-the-art baselines like Erased Stable Diffusion and Forget-Me-Not.

Our Proposed Question

Is there a principled approach for effective MU in both classification and generation tasks?
\[\hat{\epsilon}_{ oldsymbol{ heta}}(\mathbf x_t | c) = (1-w) {\epsilon}_{ oldsymbol{ heta}}(\mathbf x_t | \emptyset ) + w {\epsilon}_{ oldsymbol{ heta}}(\mathbf x_t | c )\] \[\mathbf m_{\mathrm{S}} = \mathbf 1 \left ( \left | abla_{ oldsymbol{ heta}} \ell_{\mathrm{f}} ( oldsymbol{ heta}; \mathcal{D}_\mathrm{f}) \left . ight |_{ oldsymbol{ heta} = theta_{\mathrm{o}} } ight | \geq \gamma ight )\] \[ theta_{\mathrm{u} = \underbrace{\mathbf m_{\mathrm{S}} \odot oldsymbol{ heta}}_ ext{salient weights} + \underbrace{ (\mathbf 1 - \mathbf m_{\mathrm{S}}) \odot hetafull }_ ext{original weights}\] \[ ext{Classification: } \ell_\mathrm{f} ( oldsymbol{ heta} ; \mathcal D_\mathrm{f}) = \mathbb E_{(\mathbf x, y) \sim \mathcal D_\mathrm{f}} [ \ell_{\mathrm{CE}}( oldsymbol{ heta}; \mathbf x ,y)]; ext{Generation: } \ell_\mathrm{f} ( oldsymbol{ heta} ; \mathcal D_\mathrm{f}) = \ell_\mathrm{MSE}( oldsymbol{ heta}; \mathcal D_\mathrm{f})\] \[\displaystyle ext{minimize}_ theta_{\mathrm{u}} ~ L_ ext{SalUn}^{(1)} ( oldsymbol{ heta}_\mathrm{u}) \mathrel{\mathop:}= \mathbb E_{(\mathbf x, y) \sim \mathcal{D}_\mathrm{f}, y^\prime eq y} \left [ \ell_\mathrm{CE}( oldsymbol{ heta}_\mathrm{u}; \mathbf x, y^\prime) ight ]\] \[\displaystyle ext{minimize}_ theta_{\mathrm{u}} ~ L_ ext{SalUn}^{(2)} ( oldsymbol{ heta}_\mathrm{u}) \mathrel{\mathop:}= \mathbb{E}_{(\mathbf x, c) \sim \mathcal D_\mathrm{f}, t, \epsilon \sim \mathcal{N}(0,1), c^\prime eq c } \left [ \| \epsilon_ theta_{\mathrm{u}}(\mathbf x_t | c^\prime) - \epsilon_ theta_{\mathrm{u}}(\mathbf x_t | c) \|_2^2 ight ] + \alpha \ell_\mathrm{MSE}( theta_\mathrm{u}; \mathcal D_\mathrm{r})\]

author.name: Chongyu Fan[1]*, Jiancheng Liu[1]*, Yihua Zhang[1], Dennis Wei[2], Eric Wong[3], Sijia Liu[1,2]

entry.summary: Figure 1. Schematic overview of comparing conventional unlearning methods with our proposal (SalUn) in the context of mitigating the influence of the harmful concept of ‘nudity’ in DM.
